serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Hazarwi Chicken Kebab is approximately 250 g (6 pieces), typically accompanied by salad and mint chutney.

nutrient_source.title: Hazarwi Chicken Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (cr_id G00010656)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo canonical record G00010656 with confidence 0.95. All per-100 g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record. Saturated fat is estimated from ICMR-NIN tandoori chicken reference (see notes).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on tandoori chicken / high-protein low-carbohydrate grilled meat
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hazarwi Chicken Kebab. GI estimated at 30 (low) based on the analogous food category 'grilled/tandoori chicken with yogurt marinade' from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Carbohydrate content is 5.24 g/100 g; GL per 250 g serving = (30 × 5.24 × 2.5) / 100 = 3.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Hazarwi Chicken Kebab due to its very high sodium content of 1080 mg/100 g; if consumed, portion should not exceed 50–75 g and no additional sauces or salt should be added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 5.24 g/100 g with sugar 2.85 g/100 g; high protein and fat slow gastric emptying and blunt glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hazarwi Chicken Kebab is low in carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g, GI ~30) and poses minimal glycaemic risk for people with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the high fat content (26.4 g/100 g) warrants portion control to avoid excess caloric intake, and sugary dipping sauces should be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and very high sodium (1080 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hazarwi Chicken Kebab carries a high cardiovascular concern due to its combination of elevated saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), trace trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and very high sodium (1080 mg/100 g); individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should consume this dish only occasionally and in small portions, requesting reduced-salt preparation where possible.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~8.5 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; high sodium (1080 mg/100 g) worsens tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Hazarwi Chicken Kebab due to high saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g) and very high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), both of which promote systemic inflammation; substituting cream with low-fat yogurt and reducing ghee in preparation would lower the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hazarwi Chicken Kebab is a North-West Frontier (Hazara region) style tandoori chicken kebab popular in Indian restaurants across Singapore. The dish consists of bone-in or boneless chicken pieces marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture containing ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, garam masala, chilli, lemon juice, and ghee or oil, then skewered and cooked at high heat in a clay tandoor oven. The result is a smoky, charred exterior with a juicy, tender interior and a complex spiced aroma. In Singapore, it is commonly served as a set of six pieces accompanied by fresh salad and mint chutney. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 302 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 26.4 g fat (of which approximately 8.5 g is saturated), 5.24 g carbohydrate, and 1080 mg sodium. The very high sodium content makes it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease without strict portion control. The low carbohydrate content (GI ~30) makes it relatively safe for glycaemic management in Type 2 Diabetes, though the high fat density requires portion awareness. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore establishments and is gluten-free by default when no flour-based marinades are used.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo canonical record G00010656 with a stated confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and provenance is canonical. Saturated fat is estimated from ICMR-NIN tandoori chicken reference data due to absence in the HPB record; this is the only estimated field.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (8.5 g/100 g) is estimated from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (tandoori chicken, grilled with yogurt and ghee marinade) because the HPB source record does not report saturated fat separately. This is the only field not directly sourced from HPB G00010656. 2. Trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is carried forward from the input health_suitability narrative; it is consistent with trace trans fat expected from ghee-based marinades and is treated as a plausible estimate. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific dish preparation is not available in HPB Foodo G00010656 or ICMR-NIN as of 2026. 4. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this specific dish is not reported in the HPB source record. 5. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields is not available in the source record for this dish. 6. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is a confirmed zero from the approved source (the marinade and chicken contain negligible fibre). 7. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate based on grilled/tandoori chicken with yogurt marinade from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Hazarwi Chicken Kebab exists in any approved source. GL per 250 g serving = (30 × 5.24 × 2.5) / 100 = 3.9 g. 8. This dish is classified as Indian cuisine; multilingual names are provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per the Singaporean dish multilingual requirement.
